task_completion.description: Open the map layout window in the GIS software for the 'countries_base' project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIS software (e.g., gvSIG) is open, and a layout/print view window is active, distinct from the standard map view.

task_completion.description: Insert a map view frame displaying the 'Vista' view of world countries.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large frame within the layout page contains the map of world countries.

task_completion.description: Add a title text element reading 'World Countries Map'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text box or label is visible on the layout containing the exact text 'World Countries Map'.

task_completion.description: Add a legend to the map layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A legend element is present on the layout page, showing the symbology/colors used for the map layers.

task_completion.description: Add a scale bar and a north arrow to the layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Both a graphical scale bar and a north arrow symbol are placed on the layout page.

task_completion.description: Export the map layout as a PNG image to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog or file manager shows the layout being saved as a PNG file to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/world_map_layout.png'.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIS software's graphical user interface to construct the layout.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through layout tools, inserting elements via menus or toolbars, rather than using terminal commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The layout elements (legend, scale bar, north arrow) are generated by the GIS software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The elements appear as native GIS layout objects with standard software styling, not as images copied and pasted from a web browser or external image editor.

integrity.description: The agent actually exported the layout from the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Export to Image' or 'Save as' dialog of the GIS software is visible, confirming the file was generated from the layout and not just moved/renamed from an existing file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The exported map layout must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/world_map_layout.png'. The layout must contain exactly 5 required elements: a map view frame, a legend, a scale bar, a north arrow, and a title. The title text must read exactly 'World Countries Map'. The final exported image is expected to be at least 800x600 pixels.
